title: tephroseris integrifolia subsp. integrifolia
intro: Tephroseris integrifolia subsp. integrifolia, also known as the mountain everlasting, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) that is native to mountainous regions in Europe. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of Tephroseris integrifolia subsp. integrifolia include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive problems, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be consumed as a dried herb or used topically in the form of a salve or ointment.
